compiled_state.title: 818-bug dry-run coverage harness, p95 benchmarks, FP auto-fallback scope contract, and SC13 workflow-restart analysis
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Scope
Build the 818-bug dry-run coverage harness, p95 latency benchmarks per validator, FP auto-fallback scope contract and mid-workflow engagement integration test, and SC13 workflow-restart-rate analysis against the Story-1 baseline.

## Done Definitions
- **Coverage harness** replays 818-bug corpus fixture through the manifest + 5 validators; counts preventions (bugs that would have been blocked or caught).
- **SC9 threshold**: ≥100 preventions required to pass epic-closure gate; threshold enforced as an epic-closure check.
- **p95 benchmarks** measured per validator entry point (5 stages) + per consumer read (4 consumers); results published in Story 7 contract doc. Budget targets negotiated and pinned in Story 7.
- **FP auto-fallback scope contract pinned**: when measured FP rate > 10% on a rolling window, fallback engages. Scope: (1) **per-write**, not retroactive — already-written standard/deep events remain honored and are NOT truncated or re-interpreted; (2) **new events only** — classifier's next-emission on that ticket degrades to minimal tier; (3) **per-ticket scope** — fallback is per-ticket (not global, not per-epic); (4) **validators stay depth-agnostic** regardless of fallback state — depth-agnosticism (Story 2 contract) is the invariant that makes fallback safe.
- Integration test: FP rate crosses threshold mid-workflow; verify (a) already-written standard/deep events still validate, (b) new writes on affected tickets emit minimal-tier, (c) validators continue to accept all depth tiers.
- **SC13 analysis**: compare post-epic workflow-restart rate against Story 1 baseline; compute drop; documented methodology (confidence interval, sample size, contamination controls).
- Epic-closure gate reports SC9 coverage, SC14 FP rate, and SC13 restart-drop.

## Considerations
- [Testing] 818-bug corpus fixture may exceed 50MB — if so, store in Git LFS with lock; otherwise version in repo.
- [Reliability] FP fallback engagement is observable from the PRECONDITIONS events themselves — emit a fallback-engaged marker in the next minimal-tier event so operators can trace transitions.
- [Measurement] SC13 baseline was captured in Story 1 before Story 2 validator landed, ensuring uncontaminated control.
